commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Forecast items lack hover states to indicate they are distinct data points.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover effect (e.g., background-color change or slight lift) to the forecast item stack containers.

issues.description: The 'icon' property for the main condition is missing from the interface and display.
issues.fix: Update the Props interface to accept an icon prop and display it alongside the condition text.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and makes excellent use of the design system primitives. It effectively handles the data requirements with good fallback defaults; adding interactive feedback on forecast items and mapping an icon to the main weather condition would make it truly production-grade.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid structure and good use of design tokens, but lacks interactive polish (no hover states, transitions) and shows fallback placeholder values (0°/0°, '---', '☁️') when forecast data is missing, which undermines the 'all data from props' requirement. The layout is responsive but could better handle edge cases like empty forecasts.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

issues.description: The logic for the review step is hardcoded as step 5, while the prompt originally requested a 4-step survey form.
issues.fix: Adjust the progress logic to integrate the review as a view rather than a standalone step, or strictly adhere to the 4-step structure if requested.

issues.description: Missing aria-labels or group legends for the Checkbox group and Radio group components.
issues.fix: Ensure components that handle collections of inputs have appropriate fieldset/legend or aria-label attributes for screen readers.

issues.description: Validation for 'Next' button state is only implemented for Step 1.
issues.fix: Add logic to ensure the 'Next' button is disabled if required fields in Steps 2 and 3 are empty.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and uses the design system primitives effectively. The flow is logical and the code is clean, though it lacks robust form validation across all steps and could benefit from better ARIA support for group inputs.

evaluation.critique: The component has strong data presentation and hierarchy with clear step progression and proper form organization. However, there are inconsistencies in design token usage (hardcoded fallback values like '24px', '8px', '16px' rather than pure token references) and missing interactive polish on form inputs (no focus/hover states defined). The layout is solid but could benefit from better responsive handling of the horizontal button stack on mobile.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: Task title editing functionality is missing from the task cards despite being requested.
issues.fix: Add an edit button or double-click handler on the task title that toggles an inline input field.

issues.description: The 'Move to' select dropdown triggers an update on every change, but there is no visual feedback that a task was successfully moved.
issues.fix: Add a subtle loading state or brief toast/notification after the move operation completes.

issues.description: The 'Move to' select component is inside a card and does not have an aria-label, which might be redundant or unclear for screen readers.
issues.fix: Provide a descriptive aria-label for the Select component (e.g., 'Move task [title] to another column').

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, uses design tokens correctly, and implements the core Kanban requirements effectively. The layout is clean and professional; adding inline title editing would bring it to a production-ready standard.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid kanban structure with proper column layout and spacing, but uses inconsistent fallback values (e.g., 'var(--ggui-spacing-6, 24px)') instead of relying on design tokens, and lacks important interactive polish like edit-on-double-click, hover states, and visual feedback for drag operations. The data presentation is generally good but the fixed 320px column width may cause responsiveness issues on smaller screens.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Grid cells lack sufficient distinction when multiple categories are present; the legend is helpful but the layout could be tighter.
issues.fix: Add subtle box-shadow or border intensity changes on hover to make selection clearer.

issues.description: Inline styles used for hover state transforms (onMouseOver/Out) are less performant and less idiomatic than CSS classes.
issues.fix: Move hover effects to a CSS class or styled-component if supported by the library, or use a Framer Motion approach.

issues.description: The search input lacks a visual label and relies on a prop label, but keyboard navigation for the grid is not explicitly handled.
issues.fix: Use a proper form label and implement arrow key navigation for the 18-column grid to improve keyboard accessibility.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and implements the layout requirements accurately using CSS grid. It is highly functional, though adding keyboard navigation for the table and standardizing state-driven styling would move it to a production-grade level.

evaluation.critique: The component correctly implements a grid-based periodic table with proper responsive structure and good data handling. However, there are issues with design token consistency: inline style object uses hardcoded fallback values instead of pure token references, and some spacing/sizing uses raw pixel values (height: '60px') rather than tokens. Polish could be improved with focus states and keyboard navigation for accessibility.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

issues.description: Hardcoded styles for border colors and container height conflict with the requirement to use design tokens.
issues.fix: Use a dedicated border primitive or design system theme variables for the container borders, and avoid fixed height (e.g., 400px) in favor of flexible layout properties.

issues.description: The Input component lacks an explicit 'id' association with the label, and the message list could benefit from better ARIA labeling.
issues.fix: Pass a unique id to the Input component and add 'aria-label' or 'aria-labelledby' to the scroll container to distinguish it as a chat log.

issues.description: The message grouping logic is partially implemented but timestamp display is repetitive for grouped messages.
issues.fix: Ensure the timestamp is only displayed once per message group or alongside the sender name to reduce clutter.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and effectively implements the core requirements, including the grouping logic and auto-scroll behavior. While it performs well, it relies on some inline styles and hardcoded dimensions that could be better abstracted into the provided design system primitives.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid layout and hierarchy with proper scrolling, message alignment, and input area structure. However, it mixes design tokens inconsistently—some colors use tokens with fallbacks (good), but hardcoded pixel values (16px, 12px, 400px) bypass the spacing token system. The polish is good with keyboard handling and disabled states, but could benefit from loading indicators and more granular focus states.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

issues.description: The card component border does not dynamically change color to match the stock performance as requested.
issues.fix: Pass a dynamic style or variant to the Card component's border property based on the `isPositive` flag.

issues.description: Inline CSS <style> tag used for media queries is less idiomatic than CSS modules or Tailwind utility classes when available.
issues.fix: If the design system supports a grid utility component, prefer that over manual media query injection.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and fully functional, correctly handling the data stream and displaying all requested information. It follows design system guidelines well, though the visual design could be further improved by applying the semantic color to the card borders as requested in the original prompt.

evaluation.critique: Strong component with good responsive grid implementation and proper use of design tokens. Minor issues: inline style for grid with !important fallback is inelegant, and Card padding uses hardcoded '20px' instead of a design token. Polish could be enhanced with hover states on cards and loading skeletons for initial data state.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: Lack of visual transition effects between steps.
issues.fix: Wrap the currentStep content in a CSS transition or a React transition library to provide a smoother UX when changing steps.

issues.description: The review summary uses a generic list of key-value pairs which lacks semantic structure.
issues.fix: Use a description list (<dl>, <dt>, <dd>) in the review step for better screen reader interpretation of the profile data.

issues.description: The 'Dark Theme' toggle logic technically implements the functionality but assumes string-based storage vs boolean, which might be inconsistent with the rest of the form logic.
issues.fix: Standardize the theme storage or ensure the prop contract expects a string 'dark' | 'light' clearly in documentation.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows the design system primitives, and implements all requested functional requirements efficiently. It handles form state and validation correctly for a wizard pattern; adding simple enter-key support or frame-based transition animations would bring it to a perfect 100.

evaluation.critique: The component has good structural layout and data handling, but uses hardcoded fallback pixel values in spacing tokens (e.g., '24px', '32px') instead of relying purely on design tokens, and the review step displays raw camelCase keys with naive string formatting rather than user-friendly labels. Polish is adequate but lacks subtle transitions and could improve visual feedback on form states.
